title: veronica derwentiana subsp. subglauca
intro: Veronica derwentiana subsp. subglauca, also known as the Tasmanian speedwell, is a subspecies of the Veronica derwentiana plant, a type of flowering plant native to Tasmania and southeastern Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to treat various health conditions such as skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Veronica derwentiana subsp. subglauca include its potential to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant in topical applications such as salves or ointments.
